none: Lincoln Hwy upgrade from Cicero Ave. to Chicago Road, Cook County : environmental impact statement.

This document details the Illinois State Clearinghouse’s review of Project 23 (Lincoln Highway), a proposed reconstruction of a 23-mile stretch of Route 23 from Cicero Avenue to Chicago Road. The project, funded by the Department of Transportation ($9,240,000) and State funds ($3,960,000), aims to improve transportation infrastructure and enhance connectivity within Cook County. The Clearinghouse determined that the project does not conflict with any existing State plans or policies, signifying a favorable review. The document emphasizes the project’s goal of enhancing transportation accessibility and supporting regional development within the area. It also highlights the need for a revised Letter of Intent to be submitted to the funding agency before July 24, 1977, if the project changes significantly.
